verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ) is a coat protein integral to ER-Golgi trafficking. Its sole documented localization is the endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi apparatus, where it cycles via COPII/COPI coats. No evidence of PM trafficking, no cell-state-induced surface display, no lysosomal exocytosis pathway, no documented dual localization to the PM, and no secreted or post-translationally anchored ectodomain. Ruled out: cell_state_induced (no stress/activation data), tissue_restricted_surface (not applicable), lysosomal_exocytosis (ER/Golgi protein only), dual_localization (ER-Golgi cycling documented; no PM pool), stable_surface_attachment (not secreted). No therapeutic programs target it on the cell surface.
